rationale: Both candidates independently verified the exact question the human reviewer raised (is source_outputs[0] really the parent?) but neither recommended the actual requested action (add a check/comment), so recall is low for both. Y additionally flags the failing buildkite/NPU/AMD CI checks (echoing the GT's 'fix precommit' ask) and grounds its approval in concrete caller-compatibility tracing (orchestrator.py line ranges) that is more verifiable and directly relevant than X's scattered minor nitpicks, one of which (the hardcoded 'Request 0' log) isn't even a real finding. X's items are grounded but mostly low-value test-coverage nits; Y's are fewer but more precise and better tied to what the real reviewers cared about (compatibility + CI health).
